items.briefing:
Australia's Signals Directorate (ASD) has issued a warning about "device code phishing" — a technique where scammers trick you into approving fake login requests, potentially handing over access to your accounts. Criminal groups are increasingly using ready-made toolkits to run these attacks at scale, making them more common and harder to spot. Separately, deepfake incidents at a US high school highlight how AI-generated fake images can cause serious real-world harm — a reminder this threat affects Australians too, particularly in schools and workplaces.

**Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected login approval request — even one that looks legitimate — do not approve it. Contact your IT team first.

featured.title: ASD warns of device code phishing as Proofpoint tracks growing criminal toolkits
featured.quote: ASD warning on device code phishing targets millions of Australian Microsoft 365 users directly.
